approach: behavior/API change carved from #86 and user-ratified as its own CR: add library derive orchestrators (deriveRegionCapture/deriveWorkspaceCapture) that read via the adapter and refuse on an absent optional regions seam member, so the backend-unsupported refusal decision moves off cli.ts into a portable typed CaptureUnsupportedError; cli.ts delegates and maps to its existing backend-unsupported CliError (exit 1 naming the backend), CLI observable unchanged

the two template/capture scenarios (capture-workspace-enumerate-unsupported-refused, capture-geometry-unsupported-refused) already read 'When a region/workspace capture is derived' — they describe the library surface verbatim, so the frozen .feature does NOT change. Additive impl change, freeze self-clears, no re-open; the two scenarios bind at their own node against the new orchestrator, the CLI observable stays bound via delegation

no floor expected: no frozen scenario is narrowed/rewritten/deleted (Clearance clear); the refusal is a NEW library surface enforcing an already-frozen contract, and no existing caller changes status since cli.ts already refused with the same exit 1 (Compatibility clear); no logical contradiction (Conflict clear)

leave noteTabsLeftOut's best-effort courtesy describeWorkspace read in cli.ts as-is — it must return null (no help entry) on an absent member rather than refuse, a different contract from the orchestrator's refusal

why.floor: none — the .feature diff against origin/main is EMPTY (byte-identical), so no acceptance scenario is narrowed, rewritten or deleted (Clearance clear); the CLI observable is unchanged (same backend-unsupported code, exit 1, message and fix-hint text preserved verbatim, just relocated), so no existing caller changes status (Compatibility clear); no logical contradiction added (Conflict clear). No status transition: lifecycle lives only on the root spec.md, which stays implemented — the root approval map (human-ratified original gates) is left untouched rather than downgraded to a by:agent self-assertion, and this per-CR gate verdict is recorded here in the durable ledger shard instead.
why.blast: low — an internal library orchestrator pair plus a CLI delegation; the new template-capture.ts exports (deriveRegionCapture/deriveWorkspaceCapture/CaptureUnsupportedError) are NOT in the published ./template surface (the published-surface.dist freeze test passed), so no downstream API changes; the frozen suite is untouched and the two target scenarios are made provable rather than altered.
why.novelty: low — a well-specified, user-ratified followup carved from #86 (ledger 86-node-surface-tests seq 4); the change relocates an already-existing refusal DECISION out of cli.ts into the one library seam that sees the adapter, so the two frozen template/capture scenarios (which already read 'When a region/workspace capture is derived') bind at their own node.
why.confidence: high — cold sdd-spec-judge, fresh context, ALIGNED on the RE-DISPATCH: pre-flight expected-subset now matches exactly, oracle/builder/architect all PASS, no blocker, no open markers. The judge independently re-derived the governance set, ran git diff to confirm the empty .feature delta, verified the README claim true at the call site, and RAN the tests (template-capture 18/18 incl. the two new library bindings; cli 242/242 incl. the CLI sibling refusals still green through the delegation). The first dispatch's blocked verdict was a governance-preflight-missing relay defect (correction logged, correction-kind judge-iteration), not a content defect. pnpm verify green (845+8 tests).

summary: Unspecified exit-code corner surfaced by the impl-judge (architect observation): moving the geometry-capability refusal from before target-resolution (old cli.ts, exit 1 backend-unsupported) to inside the derive orchestrator (which runs after target-resolution) flips the exit code for the combined 'no pane to capture around AND backend lacks the geometry seam' case — old behavior exited 1 (backend-unsupported), new exits 2 (missing --from usage error). No frozen scenario pins this combination and no test exercises it, so neither the old nor the new order is the contract. The new order is arguably more correct (a pane is needed before the backend can be asked about geometry) and preserving the old order would reintroduce the two-place duplication this CR removed. Decide deliberately: add a scenario pinning exit-2-wins (or exit-1-wins), or document the combination as intentionally unspecified.
evidence: impl-judge confirmed no frozen scenario combines the two conditions: template-capture-no-pane-refuses uses an intact-geometry tmux adapter; backend-no-geometry/no-tabs both supply a valid pane. Latent behavior change with no scenario either way.

why.floor: none — no frozen scenario narrowed/rewritten/deleted (the .feature diff is empty; Clearance clear), the backend-unsupported CliError code/message/fix-hint/exit-1 are byte-identical to origin/main so every existing caller's observable is preserved for all specified scenarios (Compatibility clear), no logical contradiction (Conflict clear). No status transition: root spec.md stays implemented; the human-ratified root approval map is left untouched and this per-CR impl verdict is recorded here in the durable ledger shard.
why.blast: low — two new internal library orchestrators wrapping the existing pure captures, plus a CLI delegation replacing the inline capability guard; no published API change (new exports internal to template-capture.ts, published-surface.dist freeze test green), frozen suite untouched.
why.novelty: low — relocates an already-existing refusal decision into the one seam that sees the adapter; the two frozen scenarios become provable at their own node.
why.confidence: high — cold sdd-impl-judge, fresh context, re-derived every scenario's oracle from source (ADR-0016). IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true, 7/7 scenarios pass (2 new library refusals + 5 CLI siblings green through the delegation). The judge MUTATION-tested the two new library tests: swapped the capability tag, replaced CaptureUnsupportedError with a generic Error, and injected a fall-back-to-region-read bug into deriveWorkspaceCapture — all three mutants caught, so the tests discriminate. Error strings confirmed byte-identical vs git show origin/main. Overwrite-refusal and name-validation refusal confirmed to short-circuit BEFORE the orchestrator is reachable (no region read). No absorption/scope findings, no Given literal special-cased, noteTabsLeftOut left as-is, no published-surface leak. pnpm verify green (845+8). One architect OBSERVATION (exit-code ordering corner) routed as a backlog followup, not acted on silently. Self-asserted within the auto-all leash (by user).
